what_it_does: Renders the Asset Lab workspace UI: a browsable catalog of reusable visual assets grouped by category, with header actions and a Vi note, and sets the global ViContext workspace to 'asset' on mount.
observations: Uses a destructuring alias useEffectA for React.useEffect.
A static groups array defines 6 categories and 31 asset entries, each with a filename and subtitle.
Asset preview thumbnails are generated via inline style background gradients chosen by substring matching on the asset filename.
One asset ('lighting-night.png') is marked Missing / generate with a dashed border and a 'Generate' label.
The header includes category filter, import, and 'Generate asset' actions using Btn components.
A ViNote component is included, commenting on a specific extracted asset and asking about swapping it in.
standouts: Heavy reliance on inline styles and filename-substring heuristics to pick preview gradients.
A missing asset is surfaced visually inside the catalog itself.
The Vi note ties the catalog to a specific design decision (Cost Impact mockup shadow).
conventions: Functional React component registered on the window object (global registration pattern).
Uses JSX and custom components (Btn, ViNote) assumed available globally.
References CSS custom properties for spacing, colors, and radii.
Uses inline style objects for dynamic preview backgrounds rather than CSS classes.
concerns: Filename-based conditional logic for preview styling is brittle and will break if names change.
Inline styles mixed with utility classes make styling harder to trace.
No prop types or TypeScript types for the component or data shape.
Hard-coded asset list with no search/filter implementation despite a 'By category' button.
Preview divs are decorative but have no alt text or role information.
notes: Asset Lab is a static, design-handoff-style catalog page that lists project visual assets and uses inline gradient previews keyed by filename substrings.
questions: What is ViContext and where is it defined?
Are Btn and ViNote globally registered components or imported elsewhere?
How are actual asset files resolved/loaded, since only filenames and subtitles are listed?
Is the '31 assets · 7 categories' eyebrow intentionally inaccurate (6 groups are shown)?
What does 'Generate asset' trigger — a modal, an API call, or a placeholder?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to present reusable visual material; the file does that, though it is a static catalog with interactive buttons that appear to be placeholders.
intention_signals: The file seems to be trying to create a lightweight, browsable design-asset library that bridges approved mockups and implementation, while embedding assistant commentary about asset provenance.
novelty: The inline filename-driven gradient preview generation and the embedded 'Vi note' design-decision commentary inside a workspace are somewhat unusual.
contribution: Provides a concrete, categorized inventory of visual assets and a pattern for surfacing design provenance notes directly in a workspace UI.
summary_for_embedding: A React workspace component that displays a categorized catalog of reusable visual assets with inline gradient previews, header actions, and an embedded Vi design note.
intention_for_embedding: To give the project a browsable design-asset library that connects approved mockups to implementation materials.
suggested_fields: asset_source_mockup (which approved mockup an asset was extracted from)
preview_generation_rule (the heuristic mapping asset name to preview style)
